source_jobs.title: Legal Workflow Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About us:

DeepJudge is Switzerland’s leading AI and ICT scale-up, transforming how law firms and legal departments access and leverage their knowledge. Founded by former Google search engineers with PhDs in AI from ETH Zurich, and backed by top-tier Silicon Valley investors, we are building the intelligence layer that powers the legal industry.

Every firm can license the same AI models, but no two firms share the same institutional knowledge. Decades of experience, work product, and precedent are often trapped across systems, underused and inaccessible. DeepJudge makes this expertise instantly available through world-class enterprise search and AI infrastructure, enabling firms to automate workflows, build knowledge-powered applications, and turn experience into a lasting advantage.

Our technology is trusted by many of the world’s leading law firms, including Freshfields, Gunderson Dettmer, Holland & Knight, Arent Fox, and Cozen O’Connor. Headquartered in Switzerland with a growing team across North America, we are expanding rapidly and shaping the future of how professional knowledge is discovered and applied.

At DeepJudge, we move fast, think rigorously, and care deeply about what we build. We combine cutting-edge research with close collaboration with our clients to deliver solutions that truly make an impact. If you want to be part of a team defining how AI transforms high-stakes knowledge work, this is the place to do it.

We're looking for a thoughtful and pragmatic Legal Workflow Engineer to help shape the future of AI-powered legal work. As a subject matter expert on legal workflows, you will directly contribute to building use cases for various practice areas, and also serve as a resource to our product and engineering teams. Bringing practical insight into how legal professionals work.



About the role:

This is an exciting opportunity for a lawyer who is passionate about improving how legal work gets done. You are a good fit for this role if you enjoy working with customers as well as internal teams, combining thoughtful research, structured thinking, domain knowledge, and product intuition. This position is an in-office position in our Zurich office headquarters.






What you’ll be doing:

 - Serve as a subject matter expert on legal workflows and practice-area-specific processes to act as the bridge between legal expertise and technical teams, ensuring that AI workflows are both technically feasible and legally sound.

 - Research legal use cases and identify high-impact AI workflow opportunities, working directly with clients, internal teams, and your own network to understand pain points and priorities.

 - Collaborate with product and engineering to design, test, and refine AI solutions that are both innovative and practical.

 - Contribute to prompt engineering and evaluation strategies for legal-specific tasks, using your legal expertise to improve the output quality of workflows.

 - Collaborate with the sales team to develop strategies for demonstrating the product to prospective global law firms, highlighting which aspects of the product may be most appealing to specific firms.

 - Ensure our tools align with how lawyers actually work, supporting project management, knowledge reuse, and client service delivery.

 - Facilitate discovery sessions and feedback loops with clients to ensure AI solutions address real-world legal challenges.
   
   

Requirements:

 - Education: Law Degree from a Top-Tier school required and MBA preferred

 - Experience practicing law at (a) large international law firm(s), with experience working at a US firm preferred

 - Professional working experience in Legal AI

 - Deep understanding of legal workflows across practice areas, with an ability to discuss and develop both corporate and litigation use cases

 - Strong analytical and communication skills; able to translate legal context into product insight and experience in explaining legal concepts to people without a le

source_jobs.title: Applied AI Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Us:

DeepJudge is Switzerland’s leading AI and ICT scale-up, transforming how law firms and legal departments access and leverage their knowledge. Founded by former Google search engineers with PhDs in AI from ETH Zurich, and backed by top-tier Silicon Valley investors, we are building the intelligence layer that powers the legal industry.

Every firm can license the same AI models, but no two firms share the same institutional knowledge. Decades of experience, work product, and precedent are often trapped across systems, underused and inaccessible. DeepJudge makes this expertise instantly available through world-class enterprise search and AI infrastructure, enabling firms to automate workflows, build knowledge-powered applications, and turn experience into a lasting advantage.

Our technology is trusted by many of the world’s leading law firms, including Freshfields, Gunderson Dettmer, Holland & Knight, Arent Fox, and Cozen O’Connor. Headquartered in Switzerland with a growing team across North America, we are expanding rapidly and shaping the future of how professional knowledge is discovered and applied.

At DeepJudge, we move fast, think rigorously, and care deeply about what we build. We combine cutting-edge research with close collaboration with our clients to deliver solutions that truly make an impact. If you want to be part of a team defining how AI transforms high-stakes knowledge work, this is the place to do it.



About the role:

We are looking for an Applied AI Engineer to join our team. You will work directly with customers to understand how they operate, identify high-leverage opportunities, and build AI-powered solutions that solve real problems. A big part of the role is using our own platform and internal tooling to turn the product’s capabilities into high-value experiences for customers. This is not a narrow research role or a pure implementation role. This is a role for someone who is curious, resourceful, and is eager to build great solutions on top of the DeepJudge platform.



Your responsibilities will include:

 - Owning customer-specific AI solutions end-to-end, from understanding the problem and shaping the approach to building, deploying, evaluating, and improving the solution over time

 - Building on top of our platform and internal tooling to create reusable AI-powered solutions that generalize across customers and can be relied on by thousands of end users

 - Working closely with engineering and ML to improve AI-powered features of the core product and make the platform more intelligent over time

 - Helping customers and their technical or innovation teams get the most out of the DeepJudge through hands-on collaboration, guidance, tooling, and documentation

 - Evaluating new LLM models, techniques, and approaches as they emerge, and bringing them into production

 - Proactively spotting opportunities and issues, and coordinating across teams to move important work forward quickly
   
   

What we are looking for:

 - You have built technically ambitious things that use LLMs, whether in personal or professional projects, through APIs directly, frameworks such as DSPy, LangChain, or LangGraph, or equivalent systems built from scratch

 - Proficiency in Python or JavaScript

 - You are an exceptional communicator, both written and verbal, and you build trust quickly with technical and non-technical stakeholders

 - You are highly customer-oriented, have strong intuition for what creates value, and care deeply about what you ship

 - You are a persistent problem-solver who is comfortable in fast-moving environments where you need to figure things out as you go

 - You do not need a legal background, but you should be excited to develop real domain expertise over time

 - We care much more about your ability, judgment, and potential than about a specific number of years of experience
   
   

What you can look forward to:

 - Taking ownership of meaningful work from day one

 - Building

source_jobs.title: Technology Partner Manager
source_jobs.description:
About us:

Founded by former Google search engineers with PhDs in AI from ETH Zurich, DeepJudge helps legal teams unlock and apply the knowledge inside their organizations through world-class enterprise search and AI infrastructure – enabling them to automate workflows, build knowledge-powered applications, and turn institutional knowledge into a lasting advantage.

Every organization can license the same AI models, but no two organizations share the same institutional knowledge. Decades of experience, work product, and precedent are often fragmented across systems, underused, and inaccessible. DeepJudge makes this expertise instantly available and actionable – helping legal teams find the right information faster, surface relevant knowledge, and apply it where it matters most.

Our platform is trusted by many of the world’s leading law firms and legal teams, including Holland & Knight, Gunderson Dettmer, Cozen O’Connor, ArentFox Schiff, CMS Switzerland, Schoenherr, and others.

Headquartered in Switzerland with a growing team across North America and Europe, DeepJudge is shaping the future of how professional knowledge is discovered and applied.

At DeepJudge, we move with urgency, think rigorously, and work closely with our clients to build products that solve meaningful problems. If you want to help define how AI transforms professional knowledge work, we’d love to hear from you.

 

About the role:

We are looking for a Technology Partner Manager who wants to become part of a world-class AI / Legal Tech startup. You will work closely with cross-functional stakeholders across Sales, Product, Engineering, Marketing, and Legal to help build and scale our technology partnership ecosystem. In this role, you will focus on identifying, negotiating, activating, and cultivating strategic technology partnerships that expand DeepJudge’s reach, strengthen our market position, and contribute to pipeline growth.

 

Your responsibilities will include:

 - Identify, evaluate, and prioritize technology partners whose platforms, integrations, and market presence can strengthen DeepJudge’s ecosystem and accelerate go-to-market impact

 - Lead partnership conversations from initial outreach through negotiation and signature, moving quickly while balancing commercial value, strategic fit, and long-term relationship health

 - Activate newly signed partners by coordinating internally across Product, Engineering, Marketing, Sales, and Legal to turn agreements into real launches, joint value propositions, and operational momentum

 - Cultivate existing partnerships by developing joint plans, maintaining strong partner relationships, and driving ongoing engagement after signature rather than treating the deal as the finish line

 - Help define how DeepJudge works with partners in a rapidly evolving vertical, navigating ambiguity with sound judgment and professionalism

 - Surface market feedback and partner insights to internal teams so that product strategy, integration priorities, and partner motions stay aligned with customer demand

 - Contribute to measuring partner success across marketing contribution, sales pipeline influence, and broader go-to-market impact as our partnerships program continues to mature

 - Bring proactive ideas on how DeepJudge can deepen its strategic position in legal AI through partnerships, integrations, and differentiated ecosystem execution

 

You're a great fit if you:

 - You have 3+ years of experience in partnerships, business development, or a related commercial role in B2B SaaS

 - You have a strong commercial mindset and understand how partnerships and integrations can create meaningful go-to-market impact, not just technical outcomes

 - You are an excellent relationship builder and negotiator, able to work effectively with external partners as well as internal stakeholders across product, legal, engineering, and sales

 - You are comfortable operating in ambiguity, especially in fast-moving markets wher

source_jobs.title: Open Application
source_jobs.description:
About Us:

DeepJudge is Switzerland’s leading AI and ICT scale-up, transforming how law firms and legal departments access and leverage their knowledge. Founded by former Google search engineers with PhDs in AI from ETH Zurich, and backed by top-tier Silicon Valley investors, we are building the intelligence layer that powers the legal industry.

Every firm can license the same AI models, but no two firms share the same institutional knowledge. Decades of experience, work product, and precedent are often trapped across systems, underused and inaccessible. DeepJudge makes this expertise instantly available through world-class enterprise search and AI infrastructure, enabling firms to automate workflows, build knowledge-powered applications, and turn experience into a lasting advantage.

Our technology is trusted by many of the world’s leading law firms, including Freshfields, Gunderson Dettmer, Holland & Knight, Arent Fox, and Cozen O’Connor. Headquartered in Switzerland with a growing team across North America, we are expanding rapidly and shaping the future of how professional knowledge is discovered and applied.

At DeepJudge, we move fast, think rigorously, and care deeply about what we build. We combine cutting-edge research with close collaboration with our clients to deliver solutions that truly make an impact. If you want to be part of a team defining how AI transforms high-stakes knowledge work, this is the place to do it.



Can’t find the right job opening?

If you’re excited about DeepJudge and our mission but haven’t found a current opening that matches your background, we’d still love to hear from you. Our Open Application allows you to introduce yourself and share how your expertise and interests could contribute to our journey.

Please note that this Open Application is intended for full-time positions only. Opportunities for working students, internships, or part-time roles are published separately when available.

By submitting an Open Application, you acknowledge that DeepJudge may retain your application materials and consider you for current and future employment opportunities that align with your experience and interests. DeepJudge processes personal data in accordance with its Privacy Policy https://www.deepjudge.ai/privacy-policy and applicable data protection laws. Applicant data will be retained only as long as necessary for recruitment purposes and may be deleted upon request by contacting legal@deepjudge.ai.


At DeepJudge, we believe great teams are built on diverse perspectives and experiences. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to fostering an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can thrive. We welcome applicants of all backgrounds and do not discriminate based on race, religion, color, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

source_jobs.title: Demand Generation Manager
source_jobs.description:
About DeepJudge:

DeepJudge is building the intelligence layer for legal work.

Founded by former Google search engineers with PhDs in AI from ETH Zurich, DeepJudge helps legal teams unlock and apply the knowledge inside their organizations through world-class enterprise search and AI infrastructure – enabling them to automate workflows, build knowledge-powered applications, and turn institutional knowledge into a lasting advantage.

Every organization can license the same AI models, but no two organizations share the same institutional knowledge. Decades of experience, work product, and precedent are often fragmented across systems, underused, and inaccessible. DeepJudge makes this expertise instantly available and actionable – helping legal teams find the right information faster, surface relevant knowledge, and apply it where it matters most.

Our platform is trusted by many of the world’s leading law firms and legal teams, including Holland & Knight, Gunderson Dettmer, Cozen O’Connor, ArentFox Schiff, CMS Switzerland, Schoenherr, and others.

Headquartered in Switzerland with a growing team across North America and Europe, DeepJudge is shaping the future of how professional knowledge is discovered and applied.

At DeepJudge, we move with urgency, think rigorously, and work closely with our clients to build products that solve meaningful problems. If you want to help define how AI transforms professional knowledge work, we’d love to hear from you.



About the Role:

DeepJudge is looking for a senior Demand Generation Manager to own and scale pipeline creation across channels and campaigns. This role reports to the Director of Marketing and sits at the intersection of marketing and revenue, translating positioning, content, and product initiatives into structured programs that drive measurable pipeline impact.

You will define campaign priorities, shape channel strategy, and ensure audiences are effectively engaged and converted across the funnel – from first interaction to qualified opportunity.

This is a hands-on role with end-to-end ownership of pipeline generation and conversion, working closely with the Director of Marketing and acting cross-functionally across marketing disciplines and the broader organization.

You’ll be joining at a time of strong momentum for DeepJudge. Following a $42.2M Series A led by Felicis, with continued support from Coatue, DeepJudge has expanded its presence across leading law firms globally. Recent product innovations, including the launch of SuperSearch, alongside strategic partnerships such as with Thomson Reuters, reflect the increasing adoption of DeepJudge across legal workflows.



Your responsibilities will include:

 - Drive pipeline generation
   
   - Own marketing-sourced and influenced pipeline across key regions
   
   - Translate business and marketing priorities into structured demand generation programs
   
   - Partner closely with sales on account targeting and pipeline development
   
   - Work cross-functionally with content, product marketing, events, and marketing operations to translate strategy into pipeline-generating programs.

 - Lead campaign strategy and execution
   
   - Plan and execute integrated campaigns across ABM, events and webinars, content, product releases, and partner marketing programs.
   
   - Turn key initiatives into coordinated, multi-channel campaigns
   
   - Maintain a balanced mix of always-on and campaign-driven efforts

 - Optimize full-funnel performance and conversion
   
   - Improve performance across the funnel throughout the entire buyer journey
   
   - Optimize key touchpoints including:
     
     - Website journeys and landing pages
     
     - Demo and high-intent entry points
     
     - Lead-to-opportunity progression
   
   - Design and scale lifecycle and nurture programs to drive engagement and pipeline velocity
   
   - Continuously test and improve conversion through structured experimentation (CRO)

 - Own channel

source_jobs.title: Backend Software Engineer -  Applications
source_jobs.description:
About us:

DeepJudge is Switzerland’s leading AI and ICT scale-up, transforming how law firms and legal departments access and leverage their knowledge. Founded by former Google search engineers with PhDs in AI from ETH Zurich, and backed by top-tier Silicon Valley investors, we are building the intelligence layer that powers the legal industry.

Every firm can license the same AI models, but no two firms share the same institutional knowledge. Decades of experience, work product, and precedent are often trapped across systems, underused and inaccessible. DeepJudge makes this expertise instantly available through world-class enterprise search and AI infrastructure, enabling firms to automate workflows, build knowledge-powered applications, and turn experience into a lasting advantage.

Our technology is trusted by many of the world’s leading law firms, including Gunderson Dettmer, Holland & Knight, Arent Fox, and Cozen O’Connor. Headquartered in Switzerland with a growing team across North America, we are expanding rapidly and shaping the future of how professional knowledge is discovered and applied.

At DeepJudge, we move fast, think rigorously, and care deeply about what we build. We combine cutting-edge research with close collaboration with our clients to deliver solutions that truly make an impact. If you want to be part of a team defining how AI transforms high-stakes knowledge work, this is the place to do it.



About the role:

At DeepJudge, we value engineers who care deeply about building high-quality systems while iterating quickly. As a Backend Software Engineer in the applications team, you’ll be building the backbone that powers our web application and its core capabilities. You will participate in design discussions from an early stage, helping shape both new and existing features, and you will soon be responsible for taking the technical lead on delivering large and complex backend systems changes.

You’ll be primarily working in our Python application backend, with occasional exposure to other services written in Rust, C++, and Go. The system consists of several microservices that are deployed into a large number of heterogeneous clusters, both in cloud and on-premises environments. A key part of the role will be reasoning about system performance and managing resources effectively across these different deployment contexts.



Your responsibilities will include:

 - Design, develop, and maintain efficient, scalable backend services using modern tools and frameworks

 - Contribute to architecture and design decisions for new and existing systems

 - Review code and contribute to a high standard of code quality and maintainability

 - Improve how we work by improving tooling, refining processes, and sharing knowledge



You're a great fit if you:

 - Have a Master’s degree in Computer Science or a related field, or equivalent practical experience

 - Have 3+ years of professional experience working as a Software Engineer, ideally in a scale-up or fast-growing environment

 - Are highly proficient in one or more backend programming languages (as well as their ecosystems) such as Python, Rust, Go, or C++

 - Are comfortable working with highly concurrent/asynchronous code

 - Have solid experience working with databases (SQL and/or NoSQL)

 - Are comfortable with modern web architectures and cloud platforms

 - Have experience owning projects end-to-end, from planning and implementation to maintenance and observability

 - (Nice to have) Have experience integrating with LLM providers and building products that make heavy use of LLMs to deliver real user value

 - (Nice to have) Have experience with streaming and/or message queue systems such as Redis Streams, Kafka, or RabbitMQ

 - (Nice to have) Experience working with Kubernetes, including deploying services and debugging running workloads in a production environment



What you can look forward to:

 - Location: Office 5min walking distance from Zurich centr

source_jobs.title: Customer Success Manager (Remote-USA)
source_jobs.description:
About DeepJudge:

DeepJudge is the intelligence layer for legal work. Founded by former Google search engineers with PhDs in AI from ETH Zurich, we help legal teams unlock the institutional knowledge trapped inside their organizations, through world-class enterprise search and AI infrastructure.

Every firm can license the same AI models, but no two firms share the same institutional knowledge. Decades of experience, work product, and precedent are often trapped across systems, underused and inaccessible. DeepJudge makes this expertise instantly available through world-class enterprise search and AI infrastructure, enabling firms to automate workflows, build knowledge-powered applications, and turn experience into a lasting advantage.

Our platform is trusted by many of the world’s leading law firms, including Holland & Knight, Gunderson Dettmer, Cozen O’Connor, ArentFox Schiff, CMS Switzerland, and Schoenherr, as well as leading Fortune 500 corporate legal teams. We are headquartered in Switzerland with a growing team across North America and Europe, backed by a $42.2M Series A led by Felicis with continued support from Coatue. Recent launches such as SuperSearch and partnerships with Thomson Reuters and Harvey reflect the increasing adoption of DeepJudge across legal workflows.

 

At DeepJudge, we move fast, think rigorously, and care deeply about what we build. We combine cutting-edge research with close collaboration with our clients to deliver solutions that truly make an impact. If you want to be part of a team defining how AI transforms high-stakes knowledge work, this is the place to do it.

 

If you are passionate about delivering exceptional customer experiences and building lasting client relationships, we would love to connect with you about joining our rapidly growing team as a Customer Success Manager. You will play a key role in transforming the way large law firms leverage technology to drive efficiency and profitability. 

 

Your responsibilities will include:

Retention & Growth: Monitor client satisfaction, identify upsell opportunities, and ensure renewals by driving ROI for our clients.

Client Onboarding: Guide new clients through successful implementation and onboarding, tailoring strategies to meet their unique needs.

Relationship Management: Serve as the primary point of contact for your portfolio of clients, fostering strong, long-term relationships.

Project & Stakeholder Management: Coordinate cross-functional projects and manage stakeholder expectations to ensure smooth execution and client success.

Presentations & Training: Lead engaging training sessions and business reviews with senior professionals.

Issue Resolution: Proactively address client concerns and resolve issues with professionalism and efficiency.

Insights & Feedback: Gather client feedback to drive continuous improvement and contribute to product development.

What we are looking for:

 - Experience: 5+ years in a customer success, client-facing, or account management role, ideally in a SaaS or legal technology environment.

 - Professionalism: Polished communication and presentation skills, with the ability to confidently engage senior legal professionals.

 - Legal Expertise (Nice to Have): A Juris Doctor (J.D.) or prior experience practicing law is highly preferred, demonstrating an in-depth understanding of legal workflows and client needs.

 - Tech-Savvy: Comfortable navigating and explaining complex software, with a keen intellectual curiosity and passion for emerging technologies, including AI and Large Language Models (LLMs).

 - Strategic & Self-Directed: A proactive and entrepreneurial mindset, with the ability to thrive in a dynamic "startup/scale-up" environment where you will help build processes rather than simply follow a pre-existing playbook.

 - Detail-Oriented: A meticulous approach to managing tasks, timelines, and client requests.

 - Responsive & Reliable: Ability to effectively manage a book of business

source_jobs.title: Enterprise Account Director – Corporate Legal
source_jobs.description:
About Us:

DeepJudge is Switzerland’s leading AI and ICT scale-up, transforming how law firms and legal departments access and leverage their knowledge. Founded by former Google search engineers with PhDs in AI from ETH Zurich, and backed by top-tier Silicon Valley investors, we are building the intelligence layer that powers the legal industry.

Every firm can license the same AI models, but no two firms share the same institutional knowledge. Decades of experience, work product, and precedent are often trapped across systems, underused and inaccessible. DeepJudge makes this expertise instantly available through world-class enterprise search and AI infrastructure, enabling firms to automate workflows, build knowledge-powered applications, and turn experience into a lasting advantage.

Our technology is trusted by many of the world’s leading law firms, including Freshfields, Gunderson Dettmer, Holland & Knight, Arent Fox, and Cozen O’Connor. Headquartered in Switzerland with a growing team across North America, we are expanding rapidly and shaping the future of how professional knowledge is discovered and applied.

At DeepJudge, we move fast, think rigorously, and care deeply about what we build. We combine cutting-edge research with close collaboration with our clients to deliver solutions that truly make an impact. If you want to be part of a team defining how AI transforms high-stakes knowledge work, this is the place to do it.



About the role:

We are looking for an Enterprise Account Director to focus on high-value accounts in the Corporate Legal segment, specifically Fortune 500 and Global 2000 companies. You will work alongside the VP of Sales to execute a sales strategy that results in revenue growth and new customer acquisition.



What you'll be doing:

 - Identify and engage potential clients, strategically qualifying leads to build a robust sales pipeline

 - Cultivate strong and enduring relationships with clients, uncovering growth opportunities and ensuring customer satisfaction

 - Plan, coordinate and execute sales presentations to inform, persuade, educate and ultimately close business

 - Attend seminars, trade shows, and other events to pitch DeepJudge and meet existing and prospective clients

 - Travel (as needed) to both existing and prospective clients, fostering market share growth and effectively promoting and selling our solutions

 - Develop and evangelize new use cases for DeepJudge within the corporate environment
   
   

What we are looking for:

 - 5–10+ years of experience in enterprise B2B SaaS sales, with a focus on Corporate Legal departments

 - US-based candidate (East Coast preferred), able to operate effectively in a fully remote environment

 - Expert at handling complex corporate decision-maker structures (GC, CIO, Procurement)

 - Ability to translate technical AI capabilities into business ROI for corporate stakeholders

 - Honest seller with a consultative, value-based mindset

 - Enterprise Search or Agentic AI experience is an asset
   
   

What you can look forward to:

 - Shaping the future of a high-impact deep-tech AI startup that impacts how corporate legal knowledge is discovered

 - A fast-paced learning path, where you grow professionally every day

 - Competitive US-market compensation

 - Flexible work arrangements and a stellar team



Compensation is determined based on role level, location, skills, and experience, and the pay of employees in similar positions. The expected compensation range is USD 220,00 OTE and USD 270,000 OTE. Eligible employees may also receive incentives and benefits as part of the total compensation package. This position is open to candidates who are legally authorized to work in the United States

 

At DeepJudge, we believe great teams are built on diverse perspectives and experiences. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to fostering an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can thrive. We welcom

source_jobs.title: Executive Assistant to the CEO/extended C-Level (6-Month Cover, Open to Permanent)
source_jobs.description:
About us:

DeepJudge is building the intelligence layer for legal work.

Founded by former Google search engineers with PhDs in AI from ETH Zurich, DeepJudge helps legal teams unlock and apply the knowledge inside their organizations through world-class enterprise search and AI infrastructure – enabling them to automate workflows, build knowledge-powered applications, and turn institutional knowledge into a lasting advantage.

Every organization can license the same AI models, but no two organizations share the same institutional knowledge. Decades of experience, work product, and precedent are often fragmented across systems, underused, and inaccessible. DeepJudge makes this expertise instantly available and actionable – helping legal teams find the right information faster, surface relevant knowledge, and apply it where it matters most.

Our platform is trusted by many of the world’s leading law firms and legal teams, including Holland & Knight, Gunderson Dettmer, Cozen O’Connor, ArentFox Schiff, CMS Switzerland, Schoenherr, and others.

Headquartered in Switzerland with a growing team across North America and Europe, DeepJudge is shaping the future of how professional knowledge is discovered and applied.

At DeepJudge, we move with urgency, think rigorously, and work closely with our clients to build products that solve meaningful problems. If you want to help define how AI transforms professional knowledge work, we’d love to hear from you.



About the role:

DeepJudge is a founder-led company. We are looking for a highly organized, proactive, and resourceful Executive Assistant to work with our CEO and the wider C-Level team. You thrive in a fast-paced, high-growth environment and are the person who makes sure nothing falls through the cracks in our CEO's world: calendar, inbox, commitments, and team needs. This role is built on trust, precision, and quiet excellence.

We are a high-growth technology company, and there will be an opportunity to expand this role into a permanent full-time role. If you want to be the force multiplier behind the leadership of the #1 AI scaleup in Switzerland, this is your shot.



Your responsibilities will include:

 - Manage our CEO's and the extended C-Level's calendars and inbox, ensuring efficient scheduling and smooth coordination across conflicting priorities, making judgment calls on what deserves attention while protecting everybody’s time

 - Take care of the operational details and administrative workflows: travel arrangements, scheduling, logistics and the hundred other things that keep the executive team’s work life running

 - Take care of company correspondence and administrative requests from the team, including interfacing with authorities and external partners

 - Help bring team events to life and keep office operations going

 - Handle sensitive information with complete discretion



You're a great fit if you:

 - Have demonstrated expertise as an Executive Assistant or similar administrative/tactical role in a deep-tech startup or scale up (and understands the flexibility that comes with it).

 - You've supported multiple executives (or executive teams)

 - Are trustworthy, diligent, and reliable at the highest level, know when to execute and when further clarification is needed, and navigate sensitive dynamics with grace and good judgment

 - Tech savvy: Google Workplace, Slack, Notion, and LLM's of choice are not new or intimidating.

 - Enjoy working on-site in our Zurich office (5min from Zurich main station)

 - Have excellent command of English (German is a plus)



What you can look forward to:

 - Acting as the force multiplier behind the leadership of the #1 AI scaleup in Switzerland

 - Sitting at the heart of our company and working closely with our CEO and wider C-Level

 - A fast-paced environment with the opportunity to learn equally as fast and earn trust over time

 - Option to evolve this role into a permanent full-time position

 - A modern office envi

source_jobs.title: Backend Software Engineer - Indexing
source_jobs.description:
About us:

DeepJudge is Switzerland’s leading AI and ICT scale-up, transforming how law firms and legal departments access and leverage their knowledge. Founded by former Google search engineers with PhDs in AI from ETH Zurich, and backed by top-tier Silicon Valley investors, we are building the intelligence layer that powers the legal industry.

Every firm can license the same AI models, but no two firms share the same institutional knowledge. Decades of experience, work product, and precedent are often trapped across systems, underused and inaccessible. DeepJudge makes this expertise instantly available through world-class enterprise search and AI infrastructure, enabling firms to automate workflows, build knowledge-powered applications, and turn experience into a lasting advantage.

Our technology is trusted by many of the world’s leading law firms, including Freshfields, Gunderson Dettmer, Holland & Knight, Arent Fox, and Cozen O’Connor. Headquartered in Switzerland with a growing team across North America, we are expanding rapidly and shaping the future of how professional knowledge is discovered and applied.

At DeepJudge, we move fast, think rigorously, and care deeply about what we build. We combine cutting-edge research with close collaboration with our clients to deliver solutions that truly make an impact. If you want to be part of a team defining how AI transforms high-stakes knowledge work, this is the place to do it.



About the role:

We’re looking for a Backend Software Engineer to join our team and take meaningful ownership from day one. You’ll work closely with smart, driven, and genuinely fun people who are passionate about tackling the complex challenges involved in building a high-performance product at scale.

Our indexing system is the backbone of our search engine, processing queries over hundreds of millions of documents in fractions of a second. Building and maintaining these indices within tight time and resource constraints requires highly performant, scalable software. To achieve this, we rely on modern technologies in distributed and parallel computing, database systems, and cloud platforms. You’ll leverage the strengths of these technologies to meet demanding throughput and latency requirements.




Your responsibilities will include:

 - Design, develop, and maintain efficient, scalable backend services using modern tools and frameworks

 - Take ownership of core components of our indexing infrastructure

 - Collaborate with other engineers to define, design, and ship new features

 - Optimize performance and scalability across our data processing pipelines

 - Integrate with third-party data providers and effectively use their APIs

 - Review code and contribute to a high standard of code quality and maintainability

 - Participate in agile development processes, including sprint planning, daily stand-ups, and retrospectives

 - Stay up to date with the latest industry trends and technologies
   
   

You're a great fit if you:

 - Have a Master’s degree in Computer Science or a related field, or equivalent practical experience

 - Have 2+ years of experience as a Backend Software Engineer, ideally in a scale-up or fast-growing environment

 - Are highly proficient in one or more backend programming languages such as Python, Rust, Go, or C++

 - Have solid experience working with databases (SQL and/or NoSQL)

 - Are comfortable with modern web architectures and cloud platforms

 - Enjoy solving complex problems and thinking about performance at scale

 - Communicate clearly and enjoy working in a collaborative team

 - Take ownership and can work independently when needed

 - Have experience with information retrieval or search systems (nice to have)
   
   
   

What you can look forward to:

 - Location: Office 5min walking distance from Zurich central station, where we primarily work on site

 - Collaborative and supportive work environment

 - Potential for professional growth and advancem

source_jobs.title: Demand Generation Manager
source_jobs.description:
About DeepJudge:

DeepJudge is building the intelligence layer for legal work.

Founded by former Google search engineers with PhDs in AI from ETH Zurich, DeepJudge helps legal teams unlock and apply the knowledge inside their organizations through world-class enterprise search and AI infrastructure – enabling them to automate workflows, build knowledge-powered applications, and turn institutional knowledge into a lasting advantage.

Every organization can license the same AI models, but no two organizations share the same institutional knowledge. Decades of experience, work product, and precedent are often fragmented across systems, underused, and inaccessible. DeepJudge makes this expertise instantly available and actionable – helping legal teams find the right information faster, surface relevant knowledge, and apply it where it matters most.

Our platform is trusted by many of the world’s leading law firms and legal teams, including Holland & Knight, Gunderson Dettmer, Cozen O’Connor, ArentFox Schiff, CMS Switzerland, Schoenherr, and others.

Headquartered in Switzerland with a growing team across North America and Europe, DeepJudge is shaping the future of how professional knowledge is discovered and applied.

At DeepJudge, we move with urgency, think rigorously, and work closely with our clients to build products that solve meaningful problems. If you want to help define how AI transforms professional knowledge work, we’d love to hear from you.



About the Role:

DeepJudge is looking for a senior Demand Generation Manager to own and scale pipeline creation across channels and campaigns. This role reports to the Director of Marketing and sits at the intersection of marketing and revenue, translating positioning, content, and product initiatives into structured programs that drive measurable pipeline impact.

You will define campaign priorities, shape channel strategy, and ensure audiences are effectively engaged and converted across the funnel – from first interaction to qualified opportunity.

This is a hands-on role with end-to-end ownership of pipeline generation and conversion, working closely with the Director of Marketing and acting cross-functionally across marketing disciplines and the broader organization.

You’ll be joining at a time of strong momentum for DeepJudge. Following a $42.2M Series A led by Felicis, with continued support from Coatue, DeepJudge has expanded its presence across leading law firms globally. Recent product innovations, including the launch of SuperSearch, alongside strategic partnerships such as with Thomson Reuters, reflect the increasing adoption of DeepJudge across legal workflows.



Your responsibilities will include:

 - Drive pipeline generation
   
   - Own marketing-sourced and influenced pipeline across key regions
   
   - Translate business and marketing priorities into structured demand generation programs
   
   - Partner closely with sales on account targeting and pipeline development
   
   - Work cross-functionally with content, product marketing, events, and marketing operations to translate strategy into pipeline-generating programs.

 - Lead campaign strategy and execution
   
   - Plan and execute integrated campaigns across ABM, events and webinars, content, product releases, and partner marketing programs.
   
   - Turn key initiatives into coordinated, multi-channel campaigns
   
   - Maintain a balanced mix of always-on and campaign-driven efforts

 - Optimize full-funnel performance and conversion
   
   - Improve performance across the funnel throughout the entire buyer journey
   
   - Optimize key touchpoints including:
     
     - Website journeys and landing pages
     
     - Demo and high-intent entry points
     
     - Lead-to-opportunity progression
   
   - Design and scale lifecycle and nurture programs to drive engagement and pipeline velocity
   
   - Continuously test and improve conversion through structured experimentation (CRO)

 - Own channel

source_jobs.title: Customer Success Manager (German-Speaking)
source_jobs.description:
About Us:

DeepJudge is Switzerland’s leading AI and ICT scale-up, transforming how law firms and legal departments access and leverage their knowledge. Founded by former Google search engineers with PhDs in AI from ETH Zurich, and backed by top-tier Silicon Valley investors, we are building the intelligence layer that powers the legal industry.

Every firm can license the same AI models, but no two firms share the same institutional knowledge. Decades of experience, work product, and precedent are often trapped across systems, underused and inaccessible. DeepJudge makes this expertise instantly available through world-class enterprise search and AI infrastructure, enabling firms to automate workflows, build knowledge-powered applications, and turn experience into a lasting advantage.

Our technology is trusted by many of the world’s leading law firms, including Freshfields, Gunderson Dettmer, Holland & Knight, Arent Fox, and Cozen O’Connor. Headquartered in Switzerland with a growing team across North America, we are expanding rapidly and shaping the future of how professional knowledge is discovered and applied.

At DeepJudge, we move fast, think rigorously, and care deeply about what we build. We combine cutting-edge research with close collaboration with our clients to deliver solutions that truly make an impact. If you want to be part of a team defining how AI transforms high-stakes knowledge work, this is the place to do it.



About the role:

If you are passionate about delivering exceptional customer experiences and building lasting client relationships, we would love to connect with you about joining our rapidly growing team as a Customer Success Manager. You will play a key role in transforming the way large law firms leverage technology to drive efficiency and profitability. As a Customer Success Manager, you’ll be the trusted partner and advocate for our clients, ensuring they receive maximum value from our software. You’ll collaborate with law firms an in-house legal departments across Europe to deliver a seamless and personalized customer experience.



Your responsibilities will include:

 - Client Onboarding: Guide new clients through successful implementation and onboarding, tailoring strategies to meet their unique needs.

 - Relationship Management: Serve as the primary point of contact for your portfolio of clients, fostering strong, long-term relationships.

 - Project & Stakeholder Management: Coordinate cross-functional projects and manage stakeholder expectations to ensure smooth execution and client success.

 - Presentations & Training: Lead engaging training sessions and business reviews with senior professionals.

 - Issue Resolution: Proactively address client concerns and resolve issues with professionalism and efficiency.

 - Insights & Feedback: Gather client feedback to drive continuous improvement and contribute to product development.

 - Adoption & Growth: Expansion Monitor client satisfaction, identify upsell opportunities, and ensure renewals by driving ROI for our clients.



What we are looking for:

 - Experience: 3+ years in a customer success, client-facing, or account management role, ideally in a SaaS or legal technology environment.

 - Professionalism: Polished communication and presentation skills in both German and English, with the ability to confidently engage senior legal professionals.

 - Legal Expertise (Nice to Have): A Juris Doctor (J.D.) or prior experience practicing law is highly preferred, demonstrating an in-depth understanding of legal workflows and client needs.

 - Tech-Savvy: Comfortable navigating and explaining complex software, with a keen intellectual curiosity and passion for emerging technologies, including AI and Large Language Models (LLMs).

 - Strategic & Self-Directed: A proactive and entrepreneurial mindset, with the ability to thrive in a dynamic "startup/scale-up" environment where you will help build processes rather than simply follow a pre-exis

About the role:

We're seeking a DevOps Engineer to join our team who will take ownership from day one and is eager to work alongside smart, dedicated, and fun people passionate about solving the complex challenges of building our product.



Responsibilities:

 - Manage complex cloud and on-premise deployments, setting up new deployments and optimizing existing deployments.

 - Collaborate with customers to understand their technical requirements, provide solutions to complex challenges, and implement these requirements in collaboration with our engineering team.

 - Plan, coordinate and execute implementation project plans from initiation through completion, ensuring timelines and customer expectations are met.

 - Provide technical support during the sales process, including answering and resolving technical queries and assisting with the completion of security questionnaires.

 - Assist in creating technical documentation, such as product manuals, FAQs, and integration guides.

 - Stay abreast of industry trends, competitive landscape, and technological advancements in enterprise search and AI.
   
   

Requirements:

 - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field.

 - 3+ years of experience in a technical implementation role, preferably in enterprise software, legal technology, search, document management, or related AI sectors.

 - Understanding of Linux system administration, Kubernetes deployments, database administration, and/or Software engineering.

 - Strong Proficiency in articulating complex technical concepts to non-technical audiences.Proficiency in programming languages such as Python, Golang or Java.

 - Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, with a proven track record of building customer relationships.

 - Ability to work independently and in a team environment, managing multiple priorities with excellent attention to detail.



What you can look forward to:

 - Opportunity to work with cutting-edge technologies in the legaltech and AI space

 - Collaborative and supportive work environment

 - Potential for professional growth and advancement within the company

 - Competitive salary and equity package



At DeepJudge, we believe great teams are built on diverse perspectives and experiences. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to fostering an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can thrive. We welcome applicants of all backgrounds and do not discriminate based on race, religion, color,

source_jobs.description:

DeepJudge is Switzerland’s leading AI and ICT scale-up, transforming how law firms and legal departments access and leverage their knowledge. Founded by former Google search engineers with PhDs in AI from ETH Zurich, and backed by top-tier Silicon Valley investors, we are building the intelligence layer that powers the legal industry.

Headquartered in Switzerland with a growing team across North America, we are expanding rapidly and shaping the future of how professional knowledge is discovered and applied.

At DeepJudge, we move fast, think rigorously, and care deeply about what we build. We combine cutting-edge research with close collaboration with our clients to deliver solutions that truly make an impact. If you want to be part of a team defining how AI transforms high-stakes knowledge work, this is the place to do it.



About the role:

We’re looking for a Solution Architect to join our Sales-Engineering and Deployment team to guide customers from early technical discovery through successful implementation. Working closely with Product, Engineering, and GTM teams, you will leverage your expertise to help customers understand DeepJudge's capabilities and ensure they realize value quickly.



What you’ll be doing:

 - Lead discovery sessions and communicate solution architectures to prospects, explaining how the platform fits into their infrastructure, systems, and security models.

 - Partner with technical and business stakeholders to define objectives, requirements, integration needs, and success criteria.

 - Configure platform settings to align the solution with customer requirements during implementation.

 - Create high-quality technical collateral, architecture diagrams, integration and deployment guides, and roll-out plans.

 - Serve as a trusted technical advisor during the sales motion and subsequent roll-outs, clarifying tool capabilities, deployment models, data flows, and security considerations.

 - Collaborate closely with product, engineering, and GTM to align on customer feedback and refine solution patterns and positioning.

 - Stay current on cloud, AI/ML, search, and DevOps trends and incorporate those insights into messaging and best practices.



You might be a great fit if you:

 - Have 7–10 years of experience in Solution Architecture, Sales Engineering, or a senior customer-facing technical role within enterprise search, AI/ML platforms, legal tech, or other complex B2B SaaS environments.

 - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical discipline, or equivalent practical experience.

 - Strong foundational knowledge of enterprise software architectures, databases, modern deployment environments, and enterprise integration patterns.

 - Exceptional ability to explain complex, highly technical concepts in a clear, concise, and persuasive way to audiences ranging from law firm partners and C-level executives to IT leaders and engineers.

 - Proven track record of leading customer-facing technical projects and building strong, trusted relationships with key decision makers.



What you can look forward to:

 - The opportunity to shape how customers understand and adopt powerful enterprise search and AI solutions, influencing architectural design choices and time-to-value.

 - A collaborative environment where you partner closely with experienced engineers, produc

source_jobs.title: Enterprise Account Director – EU/DACH (German-Speaking)
source_jobs.description:
About Us:

DeepJudge is Switzerland’s leading AI and ICT scale-up, transforming how law firms and legal departments access and leverage their knowledge. Founded by former Google search engineers with PhDs in AI from ETH Zurich, and backed by top-tier Silicon Valley investors, we are building the intelligence layer that powers the legal industry.

Every firm can license the same AI models, but no two firms share the same institutional knowledge. Decades of experience, work product, and precedent are often trapped across systems, underused and inaccessible. DeepJudge makes this expertise instantly available through world-class enterprise search and AI infrastructure, enabling firms to automate workflows, build knowledge-powered applications, and turn experience into a lasting advantage.

Our technology is trusted by many of the world’s leading law firms, including Freshfields, Gunderson Dettmer, Holland & Knight, Arent Fox, and Cozen O’Connor. Headquartered in Switzerland with a growing team across North America, we are expanding rapidly and shaping the future of how professional knowledge is discovered and applied.

At DeepJudge, we move fast, think rigorously, and care deeply about what we build. We combine cutting-edge research with close collaboration with our clients to deliver solutions that truly make an impact. If you want to be part of a team defining how AI transforms high-stakes knowledge work, this is the place to do it.



About the role:

We are looking for an Enterprise Account Director to focus on high-value accounts in the EU/DACH region (excl. UK). Based in our Zurich headquarters, you will leverage our Swiss heritage to build deep trust with European law firms and corporate legal teams.



What you'll be doing:

 - Identify and engage potential clients, strategically qualifying leads to build a robust sales pipeline

 - Cultivate strong and enduring relationships with clients, uncovering growth opportunities and ensuring customer satisfaction

 - Plan, coordinate and execute sales presentations to inform, build trust, drive successful customer relationships, and ultimately close business

 - Attend seminars, trade shows, and other events to pitch DeepJudge and meet existing and prospective clients

 - Travel to both existing and prospective clients, fostering market share growth and effectively promoting and selling our tools

 - Learn, develop and evangelize new ways for DeepJudge to differentiate itself in the marketplace
   
   

What we are looking for:

 - 5–10+ years of experience in Enterprise SaaS sales, ideally in Legal Tech

 - Full proficiency in both German and English is required for this role

 - Experience selling into regulated industries or legal departments in the EU

 - Proactive "self-starter" capable of working independently to open new territories

 - Enterprise search or agentic AI experience is an asset



What you can look forward to:

 - Taking on full ownership of your sales territory from day 1

 - A fun work environment in our Zurich HQ with flexible working arrangements

 - Shaping the future of a high-impact deep-tech AI startup 

 - A fast-paced learning path, where you grow professionally every day



At DeepJudge, we believe great teams are built on diverse perspectives and experiences. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to fostering an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can thrive. We welcome applicants of all backgrounds and do not discriminate based on race, religion, color, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

 

If this role excites you, but you feel you don’t meet every qualification, we encourage you to apply anyway and tell us why you’d be a great fit.

source_jobs.title: Enterprise Account Director – US Mid-Size Law Firms
source_jobs.description:

We combine cutting-edge research with close collaboration with our clients to deliver solutions that truly make an impact. If you want to be part of a team defining how AI transforms high-stakes knowledge work, this is the place to do it.



About the role:

We are looking for an Enterprise Account Director to focus on high-value accounts within the US Mid-Size Law Firm segment. You will work alongside the VP of Sales to execute a sales strategy that results in revenue growth and new customer acquisition.



What you'll be doing:

 - Identify and engage potential clients, strategically qualifying leads to build a robust sales pipeline

 - Cultivate strong and enduring relationships with clients, uncovering growth opportunities and ensuring customer satisfaction

 - Plan, coordinate and execute sales presentations to inform, persuade, educate and ultimately close business

 - Attend seminars, trade shows, and other events to pitch DeepJudge and meet existing and prospective clients

 - Travel to both existing and prospective clients, fostering market share growth and effectively promoting and selling our tools

 - Learn, develop and evangelize new ways for DeepJudge to differentiate itself in the marketplace
   
   

What we are looking for:

 - 5–10 years of experience in B2B sales, ideally selling to legal or regulated industries

 - A self-starter who thrives in a dynamic, fast-growing environmentUS-based candidate (East Coast preferred), able to operate effectively in a fully remote environment

 - Ability to work independently, prioritize, and "get things done" without an established regional template

 - Honest seller approach, consulting internally to ensure we deliver on our promises

 - Enterprise search or agentic AI experience is an asset
   
   

What you can look forward to:

 - Shaping the future of a high-impact deep-tech AI startup

 - A fast-paced learning path, where you grow professionally every day

 - Competitive compensation, flexible working arrangements, fun work environment



Compensation is determined based on role level, location, skills, and experience, and the pay of employees in similar positions. The expected compensation range is USD 220,000 OTE and USD 270,000 OTE. Eligible employees may also receive incentives and benefits as part of the total compensation package. This position is open to candidates who are legally authorized to work in the United States

 

At DeepJudge, we believe great teams are built on diverse perspectives and experiences. source_jobs.title: Working Student Quality Assurance (40-60%)
source_jobs.description:
About us:

DeepJudge is Switzerland’s leading AI and ICT scale-up, transforming how law firms and legal departments access and leverage their knowledge. Founded by former Google search engineers with PhDs in AI from ETH Zurich, and backed by top-tier Silicon Valley investors, we are building the intelligence layer that powers the legal industry.

We combine cutting-edge research with close collaboration with our clients to deliver solutions that truly make an impact. If you want to be part of a team defining how AI transforms high-stakes knowledge work, this is the place to do it.



About the role:

As a Working Student Quality Assurance, you'll join a strong team of quality assurance professionals, help maintain the high quality of our product, and ensure our customers derive the greatest possible value from it. Your primary responsibility will be to thoroughly test our key features with every product release, ensuring that our users have a seamless product experience. This is a great opportunity for a quality-driven student who approaches their work with precision and dedication.



No coding experience is required! We value your attention to detail and your dedication to quality above all else.



Your responsibilities will include:

 - Execute comprehensive test plans with precision for existing key product features and regression testing for new features to ensure a seamless product experience for our users

 - Identify, document, and report any software inconsistencies and prioritize them using our internal product planning tool

 - Provide general feedback on the usability and overall quality of the product

 - Collaborate with the engineering and product teams to ensure issues are clearly understood and resolved

 - Help maintain and update test documentation and test plans as needed

 - Participate in team stand-ups or planning meetings to stay updated on upcoming product features and improvements
   
   

You're a great fit if you:

 - Currently pursuing a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Economics, Law, Data Science, Engineering, or a related field at a Swiss University

 - Possess exceptional attention to detail, are highly organized, and have a methodical approach to tasks

 - Embrace repetitive testing work and value meticulous quality assurance

 - Have excellent command of English, both written and spoken

 - Are interested in legal technology and highly motivated to learn about the needs of our customers

 - Are available starting from Aug/Sep 2026 and can commit to working with us for at least 6 months to ensure continuity and meaningful contribution to our projects



What you can look forward to:

 - Flexible part-time hours, ideal for students

 - Opportunity to gain hands-on experience in a deep-tech scale-up

 - Be part of a strong team alongside industry experts

 - Office close to Zurich Central Station
   
   

At DeepJudge, we believe great teams are built on diverse perspectives and experiences. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to fostering an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can thrive. We welcom

source_jobs.title: Agentic Harness Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About us:

Founded by former Google search engineers with PhDs in AI from ETH Zurich, DeepJudge helps legal teams unlock and apply the knowledge inside their organizations through world-class enterprise search and AI infrastructure – enabling them to automate workflows, build knowledge-powered applications, and turn institutional knowledge into a lasting advantage.

Every organization can license the same AI models, but no two organizations share the same institutional knowledge. Decades of experience, work product, and precedent are often fragmented across systems, underused, and inaccessible. DeepJudge makes this expertise instantly available and actionable – helping legal teams find the right information faster, surface relevant knowledge, and apply it where it matters most.

Our platform is trusted by many of the world’s leading law firms and legal teams, including Holland & Knight, Gunderson Dettmer, Cozen O’Connor, ArentFox Schiff, CMS Switzerland, Schoenherr, and others.

Headquartered in Switzerland with a growing team across North America and Europe, DeepJudge is shaping the future of how professional knowledge is discovered and applied.

At DeepJudge, we move with urgency, think rigorously, and work closely with our clients to build products that solve meaningful problems. If you want to help define how AI transforms professional knowledge work, we’d love to hear from you.



About the role:

Agentic harness is the backbone of our product - it transforms powerful models into reliable enterprise-grade applications our customers can trust. You’ll join a stellar engineering team and will be responsible for the quality of our agents across applied use cases: developing the harness, crafting system prompts to optimize performance of frontier models, and building evaluation systems that keep a continuous pulse on the reliability and robustness of our features. This is a mission-critical role where your decisions compound directly into what our customers experience every day. If you believe that evals are a first-class engineering discipline - not an afterthought - and you want to own the stack that makes AI actually work in the real world, this is your seat.



Your responsibilities will include:

 - Owning the agentic harness powering critical features across DeepJudge

 - Building and running the evals end-to-end, from design to instrumentation to continuous monitoring

 - Improving agent quality through both code (harness) changes and prompting

 - Influencing our model and launch decisions from the agent quality standpoint

 - Working closely with Legal Engineering, Product, and Customer Success to shape and prioritize harness improvements

 - Stay current on LLM, GenAI, and agentic AI trends and incorporate those insights into best practices



You're a great fit if you:

 - Have a Master’s degree in Computer Science or a related field, or equivalent practical experience

 - Are proficient in one or more backend programming languages such as Python, Rust, Go, or C++

 - Know how to evaluate agentic systems and design meaningful eval frameworks

 - Have a strong interest in the latest trends in GenAI and frontier models

 - Like to build things from scratch without hand-holding

 - Have built a large application based on LLMs before

 - (Nice to have) Experience with agentic SDKs

 - (Nice to have) Familiarity with the evaluation of LLM-based systems



What you can look forward to:

 - Ownership and ability to impact the company, product, and culture from day 1

 - Front seat in one of the hottest verticals in AI

 - Location: Office 5min walking distance from Zurich central station, where we primarily work on site

 - Collaborative and supportive work environment

 - Potential for professional growth and advancement within the company
   



At DeepJudge, we believe great teams are built on diverse perspectives and experiences. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to fostering an inclusi

source_jobs.title: Production Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About us:

Founded by former Google search engineers with PhDs in AI from ETH Zurich, DeepJudge helps legal teams unlock and apply the knowledge inside their organizations through world-class enterprise search and AI infrastructure – enabling them to automate workflows, build knowledge-powered applications, and turn institutional knowledge into a lasting advantage.

Every organization can license the same AI models, but no two organizations share the same institutional knowledge. Decades of experience, work product, and precedent are often fragmented across systems, underused, and inaccessible. DeepJudge makes this expertise instantly available and actionable – helping legal teams find the right information faster, surface relevant knowledge, and apply it where it matters most.

Our platform is trusted by many of the world’s leading law firms and legal teams, including Holland & Knight, Gunderson Dettmer, Cozen O’Connor, ArentFox Schiff, CMS Switzerland, Schoenherr, and others.

Headquartered in Switzerland with a growing team across North America and Europe, DeepJudge is shaping the future of how professional knowledge is discovered and applied.

At DeepJudge, we move with urgency, think rigorously, and work closely with our clients to build products that solve meaningful problems. If you want to help define how AI transforms professional knowledge work, we’d love to hear from you.



About the role:

We're looking for a Production Engineer to help run and continuously evolve DeepJudge in production. You’ll provide incident support and build tooling, automation, and observability to keep our platform reliable and make sure inconsistencies are caught before they reach our customers.

You'll become an expert in how our systems behave under real customer workloads, using that knowledge to simplify architectures, improve reliability, and make our platform easier to operate. In doing so, you’ll play a key role in shaping the technical foundations that enable DeepJudge to scale efficiently as our customers, data, and product continue to grow.

If you enjoy solving complex distributed systems challenges, building reliable infrastructure, automating operations at scale, and continuously improving production systems, you'll thrive in this role.



Your responsibilities will include:

 - Lead technical response to production incidents by driving investigations, coordinating resolution across engineering teams, while maintaining clear communication throughout

 - Investigate root causes beyond immediate fixes, turning operational learnings into lasting improvements that strengthen the reliability and resilience of our platform

 - Build automation, internal tooling, and operational workflows that improve engineering productivity, deployment safety, and the efficiency of operating DeepJudge at scale

 - Partner with product engineers to improve the reliability, scalability, and performance of new features before they reach production

 - Help shape engineering best practices around reliability, testing, deployments, observability, and production operations as our platform and engineering organization continue to grow



You're a great fit if you:

 - Have 4+ years of experience as a Software Engineer, Production Engineer, Site Reliability Engineer, Infrastructure Engineer, or similar role operating production systems at scale

 - Stay calm under pressure and can lead technical incident response while communicating clearly and collaborating effectively across engineering teams.

 - Enjoy debugging complex problems across application code, Kubernetes, networking, databases, and cloud infrastructure, and are motivated by understanding systems end-to-end

 - Have hands-on experience with Kubernetes, containers, Linux, cloud platforms, and modern observability tooling, and know how to operate reliable production systems

 - Are comfortable writing software in languages such as Go, Python, Rust, Java, or TypeScript



What you can look forward

notes: Highly technical job postings, with a strong focus on AI and ICT. Multiple job postings for similar roles, indicating a growing team.
